---
title: "Quarto_example"
format: html
---

---

# Goals for this script:

* Brief introduction to the quarto `qmd` format.  
* Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document.  
* To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.  

---  

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

The `#| echo: false` option (placed on the first line of the chunk) disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r}
#| echo: false
1 + 1
```



--- 

$\LaTeX$ can be implemented the same way as in `Rmd` files:

* `$y_t = \beta_0 + \beta_1 x_t + \varepsilon_t$`  
* generates  
* $y_t = \beta_0 + \beta_1 x_t + \varepsilon_t$ 


--- 

#### Map plotting:

- Again, similar to Markdown, with added features  



```{r}
#| label: fig-NCarea
#| fig-cap: "NC Counties: area"
#| warning: false

library(sf)
library(ggplot2)

nc <- sf::st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"), quiet = TRUE)
ggplot(nc) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = AREA))
```


Using the `#|label: NCarea` handle, you can reference the figure in your text, by includig `@fig-NCarea` cross-reference. For example: 

* @fig-NCarea shows the area of each county in North Carolina.

---

Quarto cheat-sheet <https://rstudio.github.io/cheatsheets/html/quarto.html>
